stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 4 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 15 observations with mean 43.2 points (within-school SD = 5.4). school B has 20 observations with mean 43.6 points (within-school SD = 6.1). school C has 15 observations with mean 47.0 points (within-school SD = 3.3). school D has 10 observations with mean 51.2 points (within-school SD = 5.9). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 8.3 and 20.9 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school A: 43.2 points (shrunk from raw 43.2). Range [43.2, 43.3] as between-school SD varies from 8.3 to 20.9. Pooled mean: 45.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 45.6 points (weighted average of 4 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/15). At σ_between=13.9: B = 0.99.
Interval [43.2, 43.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (43.2) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (45.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
